description: An agent loop runs until the model decides to stop, with no step cap or terminal check, so a wrong decision loops forever.
body: Failure mode: the control loop is a while-true that exits only when the model emits a 'done' token, so a task it cannot complete spins indefinitely, calling tools and burning cost. Detection signals: no maximum step count; no explicit terminal condition checked each iteration; the only exit is model-driven; no test for a non-converging task. Do not depend on the model choosing to stop. Safer fallback: set a step cap with defined at-cap behavior, check an explicit terminal condition every iteration, and add a test that a non-converging task halts at the cap.
